content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 17, 2019

Daniel Parra
Councilman, City of Fowler
1011 Ahronian Ave
Fowler, CA 93625

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-100

Dear Mr. Parra:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") and Section 1090.¹ Please note that we are only
providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"),
and Section 1090, not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law
conflict of interest, including Public Contract Code.

Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Fresno County
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a
written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that,
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTION

Given that you are a member of the Fowler ("City") City Council and an uncompensated
member of the United Health Centers of the San Joaquin Valley ("UHC") Board of Directors, may
you take part in the City's negotiations and sale of property to UHC?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Because you do not receive income from UHC, and have no other potential economic
interest in the organization, you are not disqualified from taking part in the negotiation and sale
process as a City councilmember.

___________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-100
Page No. 2

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You are a member of the City Council. You are also currently a member of the Board of
Directors of the non-profit organization UHC. UHC is licensed by the State of California as a
community health center and designated as a "Federally Qualified Health Center" by both Federal
and State governments. UHC has 16 local healthcare centers in the San Joaquin Valley, including
one in the City of Fowler. UHC's objective is to provide comprehensive health services to the
medically underserved in the central San Joaquin Valley. UHC provides high need services to the
residents of Fowler and surrounding areas. The City has a history of providing health care services.
You are uncompensated in your capacity as a UHC Director and only receive paid travel expenses.
UHC is proposing to purchase a property from the City to expand its operations in Fowler.

ANALYSIS

The Act

Section 87100 of the Act provides, "[n]o public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest." A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or
on certain enumerated economic interests. (Section 87103.) Among these enumerated interests is
"[a]ny source of income . . . aggregating five hundred dollars ($500) or more in value provided or
promised to, received by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is
made." "Income," however, does not include "travel expenses and per diem received from a bona
fide nonprofit entity exempt from taxation under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code."
(Section 82030(b)(2).)

Given that you are not compensated for your work as a UHC Director, and the only money
received from UHC is reimbursement for travel expenses, UHC is not a "source of income" interest
and you do not have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act.

Section 1090

Section 1090 provides, "[m]embers of the Legislature, state, county, district, judicial district,
and city officers or employees shall not be financially interested in any contract made by them in
their official capacity, or by any body or board of which they are members." However, Section
1091.5 provides that an officer or employee "shall not be deemed interested in a contract if his or
her interest is . . . [t]hat of a noncompensated officer of a non-profit, tax-exempt corporation, which,
as one of its primary purposes, supports the functions of the body or board to which the body or
board has a legal obligation to give particular consideration, and provided further that this interest is
noted in its official records." (Section 1091.5(a)(7).) For the purposes of Section 1091.5(a)(7), "an
officer is 'noncompensated' even though he or she receives reimbursement from the nonprofit, tax-
exempt corporation for necessary travel and other actual expenses incurred in performing the duties
of his or her office."

File No. A-19-100
Page No. 3

As noted, UHC is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. You are an officer of UHC and you are not compensated for your work. The only money you receive from UHC is reimbursement for travel expenses related to your work. UHC's objective is to provide comprehensive health services to the medically underserved in the central San Joaquin Valley. The City also has an interest in achieving that objective, as evidenced by the City's history of also providing health care services. Accordingly, UHC's primary purpose supports the functions of the City and you have a "noninterest" in UHC as an uncompensated officer of the organization. As long as your interest is noted in the City Council's official records, Section 1090 does not prohibit you from taking part in the negotiation and sale of property to UHC.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Kevin Cornwall
Counsel, Legal Division

KMC:aja

(Section 82030(b)(2).) 
Given that you are not compensated for your work as a UHC Director, and the only money

As noted, UHC is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. You are an officer ofUHC and you

member of the United Health Centers of the San Joaquin Valley ("UHC") Board of Directors, may 
you take part in the City's negotiations and sale of property to UHC?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Because you do not receive income from UHC, and have no other potential economic 
interest in the organization, you are not disqualified from taking part in the negotiation and sale 
process as a City councilmember.
